ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: I swear by this city, Mecca -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: I do swear by this city ˹of Mecca˺—

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And you, [O Muhammad], are free of restriction in this city -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: even though you ˹O Prophet˺ are subject to abuse in this city—

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [by] the father and that which was born [of him],
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and by every parent and ˹their˺ child!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: We have certainly created man into hardship.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, We have created humankind in ˹constant˺ struggle.

ayahs.textArabic: أَيَحۡسَبُ أَن لَّن يَقۡدِرَ عَلَيۡهِ أَحَدٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ayahsabu an lan yaqdira AAalayhi ahadun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Does he think that never will anyone overcome him?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Do they think that no one has power over them,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He says, "I have spent wealth in abundance."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: boasting, “I have wasted enormous wealth!”?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Does he think that no one has seen him?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Do they think that no one sees them?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Have We not made for him two eyes?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Have We not given them two eyes,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And a tongue and two lips?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: a tongue, and two lips;

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And have shown him the two ways?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and shown them the two ways ˹of right and wrong˺?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But he has not broken through the difficult pass.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: If only they had attempted the challenging path ˹of goodness instead˺!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And what can make you know what is [breaking through] the difficult pass?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And what will make you realize what ˹attempting˺ the challenging path is?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: It is the freeing of a slave
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: It is to free a slave;

ayahs.textArabic: أَوۡ إِطۡعَٰمٞ فِي يَوۡمٖ ذِي مَسۡغَبَةٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Aw itAAamun fee yawmin thee masghabatin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or feeding on a day of severe hunger
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: or to give food in times of famine

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: An orphan of near relationship
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: to an orphaned relative

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or a needy person in misery
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: or to a poor person in distress;

ayahs.textArabic: ثُمَّ كَانَ مِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ وَتَوَاصَوۡاْ بِٱلصَّبۡرِ وَتَوَاصَوۡاْ بِٱلۡمَرۡحَمَةِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Thumma kana mina allatheena amanoo watawasaw bialssabri watawasaw bialmarhamati
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And then being among those who believed and advised one another to patience and advised one another to compassion.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and—above all—to be one of those who have faith and urge each other to perseverance and urge each other to compassion.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Those are the companions of the right.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: These are the people of the right.

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ بِـَٔايَٰتِنَا هُمۡ أَصۡحَٰبُ ٱلۡمَشۡـَٔمَةِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waallatheena kafaroo biayatina hum ashabu almashamati
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But they who disbelieved in Our signs - those are the companions of the left.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: As for those who deny Our signs, they are the people of the left.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Over them will be fire closed in.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: The Fire will be sealed over them.
